aliases: Pepperidge Farm Goldfish Whole Grain crackers

english: Pepperidge Farm Goldfish Whole Grain crackers

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term specific to wholegrain fish-shaped cheese crackers; mapped to broader cracker food product class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers crackers and crispbreads broadly; no specific code for wholegrain cheese-flavoured fish-shaped crackers exists in the published FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for this branded cracker product type.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of Goldfish Wholegrain crackers is approximately 30 g (about 55 pieces), providing roughly 140 kcal, 4 g protein, 5 g fat, and 19 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Goldfish Cracker Wholegrain — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for Goldfish cracker (wholegrain), cr_id F11080028, confidence 0.95. Values are preserved as reported.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for Goldfish Wholegrain crackers in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 67 (medium) based on the closest analogous food: whole wheat crackers / wholegrain wheat-based crackers as reported in Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283. Glycemic load per 30 g serving calculated as: (67 × 19.0 g available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 12.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 833 mg per 100 g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-safe snacks
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid Goldfish Wholegrain crackers; a 30 g serving delivers approximately 250 mg sodium, representing over 10% of the 2,000 mg daily limit, and regular consumption will substantially elevate blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High total carbohydrate (63.3 g/100 g) and sugar (11.1 g/100 g) with estimated medium GI of 67, yielding a glycemic load of ~12.7 per 30 g serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid Goldfish Wholegrain crackers; despite 6.7 g/100 g dietary fibre from whole wheat, the high carbohydrate density and moderate-to-high glycemic load will raise postprandial blood glucose, and strict portion control with protein co-ingestion is required if consumed.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very high sodium (833 mg/100 g) combined with ultra-processed formulation and moderate fat (16.67 g/100 g including saturated fat) raises cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of Goldfish Wholegrain crackers; the very high sodium content promotes hypertension and fluid retention, while the ultra-processed nature and saturated fat content are independently associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium at 833 mg/100 g exceeds the 400 mg/100 g RA threshold, promoting joint inflammation and tissue oedema
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Goldfish Wholegrain crackers; the very high sodium content exacerbates joint swelling and inflammation, and the wholegrain fibre benefit (6.7 g/100 g) is insufficient to offset the sodium burden in an RA management context.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Goldfish Wholegrain crackers are small, fish-shaped baked snack crackers made primarily from whole wheat flour and cheddar cheese, produced by Pepperidge Farm (Campbell Soup Company) and widely available in Singapore and internationally. The crackers are baked, not fried, and have a light, crispy texture with a mild savoury cheese flavour and a slightly nutty note from the whole wheat base. They are sold in various flavours but the wholegrain variant is distinguished by its use of whole wheat flour as the primary grain ingredient, contributing 6.67 g dietary fibre per 100 g. Nutritionally, they are energy-dense at 467 kcal/100 g, with a high carbohydrate load of 63.33 g/100 g (including 11.1 g sugar), moderate protein at 13.33 g/100 g, and fat at 16.67 g/100 g. The sodium content is very high at 833 mg/100 g, making this product clinically problematic for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. Despite the wholegrain base providing fibre and a lower glycemic response than refined-grain equivalents, the overall carbohydrate density and sugar content make this unsuitable for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes. The product is vegetarian but not vegan (contains dairy), not halal-certified, and contains wheat and milk allergens. A typical serving is approximately 30 g (about 55 pieces), providing roughly 140 kcal and 250 mg sodium. Common consumption contexts include children's snacks, lunchbox additions, and casual snacking occasions.
common_user_queries: Are Goldfish crackers suitable for diabetics?
Can hypertension patients eat Goldfish crackers?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Foodo Singapore database entry for this exact product (cr_id F11080028) with a reported confidence of 0.95. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. (2008) for wholegrain wheat crackers, which is the closest approved-source equivalent; this introduces minor uncertainty but does not affect the high confidence in macronutrient data.
notes: Saturated fat estimated at 3.33 g/100 g based on USDA FoodData Central reference for comparable wholegrain cheese crackers (USDA SR Legacy item 'Crackers, whole-wheat'); the HPB source does not separately report saturated fat. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific HPB Goldfish wholegrain cracker entry are not reported in the HPB Foodo database as of 2026-04-16. Calcium_mg and iron_mg are reported as 0.0 in the HPB source (confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database entry F11080028). GI of 67 is an analogous estimate based on wholegrain wheat crackers from Atkinson et al. (2008), Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283, accessed via University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Goldfish Wholegrain crackers was found in any approved source. Glycemic load per 30 g serving = (67 × 19.0 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 12.7. All core macros (protein_g 13.33, fat_g 16.67, carbohydrate_g 63.33) are non-zero and sourced from HPB. This is an American-origin branded snack product; no Singaporean, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi, or other Asian-language local names apply.
